name: Black Beauty
image_alt: Deep purple-black bell peppers on a compact plant
description: A visually dramatic sweet bell pepper with deep purple-black fruits that ripen to red. Flavor is sweet and mild throughout both stages — the black color comes from high anthocyanin content rather than any bitterness.
history: A relatively modern variety developed for the ornamental-edible market. Gained popularity among gardeners attracted to unusual vegetable colors and the dual harvest window — striking black when immature, sweet red when ripe.

good.reason: Pest deterrent for aphids and spider mites
Compatible root depth, doesn't compete heavily
Ground cover that retains moisture around peppers

seasonal_quirks: The black color is most intense with full sun and warm temperatures. In cool or shaded conditions fruits may be more purple-green than true black. Anthocyanins fade to red faster in very hot weather.
harvest_cues: Harvest black for drama and a slightly firmer texture, or leave to ripen fully red for maximum sweetness. Fruit is blocky, 3–4 lobed, 3–4 inches.
variety_notes: The anthocyanin content makes these particularly nutritious. Color provides natural camouflage from some pests that target green fruits. Compact plant habit makes it suitable for containers.
preservation: Roast and freeze. The black color fades when cooked regardless — use fresh for visual impact.
